project_type: Streamlit multi-page data analysis web app
build_system: pip with requirements.txt, no Makefile or Dockerfile
test_framework: No test framework currently - use py_compile for syntax validation
test_command: python -m py_compile app.py && for f in pages/*.py utils/*.py; do python -m py_compile "$f"; done
verification_instructions:
1. pip install -r requirements.txt
2. python -m py_compile on all .py files
3. Verify no import errors with: python -c 'import app; from utils import styles, data_engine'
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - all packages installable. No Docker. No test suite beyond py_compile.
contribution_requirements: Additive only - don't delete existing pages. Preserve session state keys. Inject CSS on every page. Gemini calls wrapped in try/except. All new session state keys initialized in app.py.
key_patterns:
- st.set_page_config() at very top of every page file (before any other st calls)
- Session state keys: df, uploaded_df, online_df, generated_report
- Gemini model: gemini-2.5-flash-preview-04-17
- Chart color: was #6C63FF, moving to #00D4FF
- utils/ for shared modules
- pages/ numbered sequentially for sidebar ordering
- All pages use wide layout
- Plotly charts use plotly_dark template
directory_structure:
Root: app.py (entry), requirements.txt, .streamlit/config.toml
pages/: numbered 1-7 (will become 1-9)
utils/: __init__.py, ai_insights.py, data_generator.py, online_data.py, report_generator.py, visualizations.py
data/: community_college_data.csv
